name: game-loader
description: Java game launcher for MMI3G — run games from SD card on the J9 JVM. Use SD slot 2 as your game library!
author: dspl1236
scripts: game_install.sh - Install game launcher to flash
game_loader.sh - Scan SD cards for available games
launch_game.sh - Launch a specific game JAR
games_included: adventure - 'The Quattro Quest' Audi-themed text adventure
notes: Games run on the J9 JVM using lsd.jxe as the Java runtime (credit: romangarms for discovering this). Games output to the GEM script console via stdout. The MMI has 800x480 display, ~40x15 character console, rotary knob + 4 soft keys. SD slot 2 can be used as a dedicated game library — just create /games/<name>/game.jar + game.manifest on any FAT32 SD card. Compatible with Java 1.4/1.5 class files (no generics, no AWT/Swing).
how_to_add_games: 1. Create a folder in /games/ on your SD card. 2. Add game.jar (compiled with -target 1.4). 3. Add game.manifest with main=ClassName and name=Display Name. 4. The game will appear in GEM > /games menu.
